channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pledge to help make Martin Luther King Jr.’s dream a reality is viewed with skepticism by many prominent Black and Hispanic civil rights leaders.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: As President Donald Trump tightens the nation's immigration policies, lawmakers in Democratic-led states are proposing new measures that could erect legal obstacles for federal immigration officials and help immigrants lacking legal status avoid deportation.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kern County reacts: Trump proposes voter ID mandate for California elections
item.description: Back when 17 News covered that story, we asked viewers if they thought voter ID should be required. More than 700 voted, and 92% said voter ID should be checked at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Department of Education dismisses 'meritless' book ban complaints
item.description: Several "book ban" complaints issued during the Biden administration have been dismissed by the U.S. Department of Education, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh approval for US’ 1st publicly funded religious charter school, in Oklahoma
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take on a new culture war dispute: whether the nation’s first publicly funded religious charter school should be allowed to open in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who's the guy handing Trump those binders of executive orders? Meet Will Scharf
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Those binders full of executive orders that President Donald Trump has been signing with a flourish and a wide-tipped Sharpie during his first week in office don't just magically appear before him.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mariann Budde, the bishop who upset Trump with her inaugural sermon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Right Rev. Mariann Budde made headlines this week after she angered President Donald Trump with her sermon during an inaugural prayer service.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders in debt limit quandary between Trump, fiscal hawks
item.description: Congressional GOP leaders are in a massive pickle as they weigh how to deal with a looming deadline to raise the debt ceiling. President Trump is pressuring Republicans to not allow Democrats to use the borrowing limit as a leverage point.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK Jr. assassinations
item.description: “Give that to RFK Jr.,” Trump said about the pen he used to sign the order declassifying info about the JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: State Supreme Court weighs what to do about ‘dysfunctional’ Minnesota House
item.description: The high court heard oral arguments Thursday over the temporary control of the Minnesota House. “This is a highly unusual situation we find ourselves in,” Justice Anne McKeig said during early questioning of attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Federal health agencies told to halt all external communications
item.description: In a memo obtained by NPR, acting Health Secretary Dorothy Fink forbade staff from public communications on most matters until Feb. 1, unless they get express approval from "a presidential appointee.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel, Selena Simmons-Duffin, and Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, Fox 5

item.title: Trump declares energy emergency
item.description: President Trump has declared an energy emergency, which his team has said would unlock additional powers to jumpstart production.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden endures painful but active last day of presidency
item.description: When President Biden walked through the Capitol on Monday with mere minutes left in his presidency, a reporter asked how he was feeling.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
